name: Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop
name_original: Los restos históricos de Alija del Infantado — Benavente y los Valles
description: Cycle 21.8 miles (35.0 km) through historical landscapes and rural scenery on this difficult touring cycling route near Alija del Infantado.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alija Del Infantado traverse historical and rural scenery within the province of León. The region is characterized by green prairies and views of small rivers and streams, offering varied terrain for cyclists. It is strategically located on the Vía de la Plata and the Camino Sanabrés, making it suitable for long-distance touring. The landscape includes historical landmarks and natural features, providing a blend of cultural and outdoor experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Alija Del Infantado The most popular touring cycling route is Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop, a 21.8 miles (35.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historical sites and the surrounding rural landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vidriales Valley and Petavonium Camp Trail, a moderate 22.6 miles (36.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Vidriales Valley and passes by the historic Petavonium Camp. Local touring cyclists also love the From Brime de Urz to the Dolmen of San Adrián — Benavente y los Valles, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through rural areas, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Alija Del Infantado is defined by historical landscapes, green prairies, and river views, with routes that cater to different ability levels. The area also features sections of the Vía de la Plata and Camino Sanabrés. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alija Del Infantado?
A: There are 6 touring cycling routes available around Alija Del Infantado, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes traverse historical landscapes, green prairies, and river views.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in the immediate guide, there are 3 moderate routes that might be suitable for less experienced cyclists or those preferring a more relaxed pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383958335/vidriales-valley-and-petavonium-camp-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley and Petavonium Camp Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.3 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384016629/from-brime-de-urz-to-the-dolmen-of-san-adrian-benavente-y-los-valles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Brime de Urz to the Dolmen of San Adrián — Benavente y los Valles&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 14.4 km moderate trail.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while cycling around Alija Del Infantado?
A: The region is rich in history. You can encounter landmarks such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, a Roman bridge over the Órbigo river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383963597/castro-de-las-labradas-and-alija-del-infantado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores historical sites and the rural landscape. You can also find the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6352862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Vía de la Plata or Camino de Santiago near Alija Del Infantado?
A: Yes, Alija Del Infantado is strategically located on sections of both the Vía de la Plata and the Camino Sanabrés, which is a branch of the Camino de Santiago. This makes it an appealing destination for long-distance touring cyclists and pilgrims. While specific routes in this guide don't exclusively follow these paths, the regional context means you'll be cycling in an area steeped in their history.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see on a touring cycling trip here?
A: The area is characterized by lush green prairies and picturesque views of small rivers and streams. You'll also find the Sierra de Casas Viejas mountain range to the south. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383958335/vidriales-valley-and-petavonium-camp-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley and Petavonium Camp Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views of the Vidriales Valley itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alija Del Infantado?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat than summer. These seasons also showcase the natural beauty of the green prairies and river landscapes at their best.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Alija Del Infantado area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383963597/castro-de-las-labradas-and-alija-del-infantado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas and Alija del Infantado Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel – Castro de Las Labradas loop from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Alija Del Infantado?
A: Alija Del Infantado itself has a town center (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt;) with popular architecture, including a Plaza Mayor, where you can expect to find local cafes, pubs, and restaurants. For accommodation, the town and surrounding rural areas offer options like hotels and rural houses.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes around Alija Del Infantado?
A: While the routes in this guide are classified as moderate to difficult, the region offers diverse terrain. For a lighter, family-friendly outdoor experience, the nearby 'El Bosque Mágico' (The Magic Forest) is a unique walking trail with imaginative decorations, which could be combined with shorter, flatter cycling sections if available locally.

Q: What are some other interesting points of interest to visit in Alija Del Infantado besides cycling?
A: Beyond cycling, you can explore the town's rich history, including its Roman traces and historical architecture. Visit the 15th-century Church of San Esteban with its Mudejar coffered ceiling, the Church of San Verísimo, or wander through the historical Jewish Quarter. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alija del Infantado Town Center&lt;/a&gt; is a great starting point.

Q: Is it possible to bring my bicycle on public transport to Alija Del Infantado?
A: Public transport options for bringing bicycles can vary by service and region. It's advisable to check directly with local bus or train operators in Castilla y León for their specific policies regarding bicycle transport, especially for long-distance journeys to Alija Del Infantado.

Q: Are there options for bike rental or repair in Alija Del Infantado?
A: While specific bike rental or repair shops are not detailed in the guide data, as a destination popular with touring cyclists and pilgrims on the Vía de la Plata and Camino Sanabrés, there may be local services available. It's recommended to inquire with local tourist information or accommodation providers upon arrival for the most current options.
tips: Ruins of a fortified settlement from the time of the Celts who fought the Romans when they arrived in this area of the Iberian Peninsula. The Castro de las Labradas is located on a steep hill and you will have to bike uphill or push.

Historic bridge over the Órbigo river that connects the provinces of León and Zamora. It is part of the original layout of the Roman road, which today is the Vía de la Plata, and has been rebuilt several times over the centuries. Specifically in the War of Independence, the English destroyed the bridge to contain Napoleon's passage from Zamora. Its current form dates from the 20th century, so it has served pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago for hundreds of years.
This small town treasures one of the most curious traditions in the area. These are mini cellars dug into the clay hill, of which you can only see a chimney and in some a small access door.
Fortification of the town of Alija del Infantado dating from the 11th century, although it has been successively reformed by the different families to which it has belonged.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 35 km (21.7 miles) long. For a touring cyclist, it generally takes around 2.5 to 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, utilizing old railway lines converted into a 'green route' or 'Camino Natural'. You'll encounter a mix of asphalt tracks, small local paved roads, and unpaved segments with gravel and dirt paths. It's predominantly flat with moderate inclines, especially between Benavente and Alija del Infantado.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the section between Benavente and Alija del Infantado is generally considered to be of low to moderate difficulty for touring cyclists, with no added difficulties. While some broader routes in the area can be more challenging, this specific segment is quite accessible.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque rural landscapes, often accompanied by the Jamuz River. Notable historical points include the ancient fortified settlement of Castro de las Labradas and the historic Puente de la Vizana over the Órbigo River. In Alija del Infantado, look out for the unique 'mini cellars dug into the clay hill' and the Castillo de los Pimentel.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal for cycling this route. The weather is generally milder, and the rural landscapes offer 'amazing colors,' especially during autumn. Summer can be hot, so plan for early morning or late afternoon rides if visiting then.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is a segment of the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road and a significant Camino de Santiago route. It also overlaps with sections of the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route connects Alija del Infantado and Benavente. Specific parking information isn't detailed, but both towns offer parking options. For a circular route, you could start and finish in either Alija del Infantado or Benavente, utilizing local parking facilities.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural areas and greenways, specific regulations regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near roads or in populated areas, and to be mindful of local wildlife and agricultural land.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, as it largely follows public greenways and local roads. Access to historical sites along the way may have separate entry requirements, but the trail itself is free to use.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Both Alija del Infantado and Benavente are towns with amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in both locations, making them convenient spots for a break or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: Is it a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The description refers to a segment of the Vía de la Plata between Alija del Infantado and Benavente. However, it's possible to create a circular route encompassing both towns, which would involve a mix of the Vía de la Plata and other local roads.
